title: [$250] Clicking "View Details" on an expense/report shows the "Hmm... it's not here" page

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
## Bug Description Clicking **View Details** from the expense/report actions menu renders the "Hmm... it's not here" (Not Found) page instead of opening the expected details page. ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Open a report or expense in New Expensify (web). 2. Open the expense/report actions menu. 3. Select **View Details**. ## Expected Result The relevant details page/panel for the selected expense or report should open, allowing the user to view the information normally. ## Actual Result The "Hmm... it's not here" page is rendered, preventing the user from viewing the details. ## Platform Web (NewDot) **Slack thread:** https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1778011018337669

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** v9.3.67-3 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** Unable to validate in Prod - Provide reasoning. **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://test-management.browserstack.com/projects/2219752/folder/13176677/test-cases/41237668 **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** N/A **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Regression TC Execution **Device used:** Windows 11, Chrome **App Component:** Other ## Action Performed: 1. Go to https://staging.new.expensify.com/ 2. Sign up using a Gmail address with a + 3. Click Skip > Manage my team's expenses > 11-50 > QBD > Continue 4. Open the console and run the following snippet (replace with today’s date and time): Onyx.merge('nvp_private_lastDayFreeTrial', '2026-05-05 01:01:00'); 5. Run the following snippet: Onyx.merge('nvp_private_amountOwed', 1000); 6. Run the following snippet: Onyx.merge('nvp_private_billingGracePeriodEnd', 1719792000); 7. Disable the internet connection on the device. 8. Click FAB > Create Expense > Manual expense > Enter amount > Next 9. On the Choose recipient page, select the workspace chat. 10. Click Add payment card ## Expected Result: After clicking Add payment card, the user should be redirected to the Subscription page. ## Actual Result: After clicking Add payment card, the user is not redirected to the Subscription page. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/249cb962-9bf0-4ba9-8d68-23d1eded6c48 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** v9.3.67-3 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** Yes **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/87737 **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** N/A **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Pull Request QA execution **Device used:** Windows 11 / Chrome, Samsung Galaxy A52 / Android 14, iPhone 13 / iOS 26.5 **App Component:** Workspace Settings ## Action Performed: Preconditions - Logged in to any user - Create a workspace with distance rates, merchant rules, taxes, categories, members, report fields data Steps: 1. Go offline 2. Delete a rule, a member, a tax, a category, a distance rate, a report field 3. Duplicate the workspace 4. Observe the pending deleted data confirmation page -> note that here the report field is counted as 1 5. Tap "continue" 6. Tap the duplicate workspace 7. Open "Reports" in the workspace setting ## Expected Result: In step 4, the pending deleted report field data shouldn't be counted. It should match what appears when opening the report in the duplicated workspace, where there are no report fields. ## Actual Result: in step 4, the pending deleted report field data is counted in confirmation page of duplicate WS ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [x] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [x] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1bd6eed0-4368-4ca4-9186-62e84ef6dc7b [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.67-0 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** Yes **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://test-management.browserstack.com/projects/2219752/test-runs/TR-25792/folder/13176933/41237726/1391376627?sort=%2Bidentifier **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** jsat@applausemail.com **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Regression TC Execution **Device used:** Macbook Pro/iOS 13.7.8 **App Component:** Other ## Action Performed: Preconditions Make sure that location permission is disabled in your browser 1. Navigate to https://staging.expensify.com/ 2. Log in with valid credentials 3. Click on FAB > Create Expense > Scan > Upload a receipt > Choose a recipient > Create expense 4. When "Allow location access" modal is displayed, click on "Got It" option 5. After Verify the expense is created, click on FAB > then click on QAB > Upload a receipt > Create expense ## Expected Result: "Allow location access" modal is displayed when creating scan receipt expense from QAB ## Actual Result: "Allow location access" modal is not displayed when creating scan receipt expense from QAB ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8e5d0067-c7c4-4478-939d-b549b5e33d19 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)
